channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Congress Retains Bagalkot Assembly Seat with Support from AHINDA Voters and Local Campaign Efforts
item.description: In the recently concluded Bagalkot Assembly by-election, the Congress party successfully retained the seat following the demise of H.Y. Meti. Umesh Meti, son of the late MLA, secured victory aided by ...

item.title: At least 13 injured in Oklahoma lake party shooting, suspects still at large
item.description: On Sunday evening, a mass shooting occurred at a campground near Arcadia Lake in Edmond, Oklahoma, leaving at least 13 people hospitalized. The incident, which took place around 9 p.m., involved two m...

item.title: Heat-related deaths in Karachi as the city hits its hottest day of the year
item.description: At least eight people have died in Karachi due to suspected heatstroke as the city recorded its hottest day of the year on Monday. Rescue officials reported that four bodies were recovered from differ...

item.title: First Ogun Pilgrims Depart from Gateway Airport for 2026 Hajj
item.description: The first batch of pilgrims from Ogun State have successfully arrived in Saudi Arabia for the 2026 Hajj. This marks a historical achievement for Ogun State, as it is the first time pilgrims are depart...

item.title: Minister places NSFAS under administration to stabilise governance and operations
item.description: Minister of Higher Education and Training, Buti Manamela, has officially placed the National Student Financial Aid Scheme (NSFAS) under administration, citing ongoing governance instability, audit fai...

item.title: Tanzania Faces Urgent Need to Adapt to Digital Media and AI
item.description: Back in 2005, Tanzanian journalist Muhidin Issa Michuzi discovered blogging during a conference in Helsinki, Finland, and realised the huge potential of digital media. At that time, most local media h...

item.title: Sydney Council Cancels Pro-Palestine Forum Over Public Safety Concerns
item.description: Sydney’s Lord Mayor Clover Moore has called off a pro-Palestine forum originally set to take place at the East Sydney Community Arts Centre. The event, organised by the Stop The War On Palestine group...

item.title: AFL hands two-game ban for tackle while investigating Lions player over alleged VFL homophobic remark
item.description: The AFL has handed Gold Coast’s Sam Clohesy a two-match ban for a dangerous tackle that caused GWS winger Ryan Angwin to suffer a concussion during their recent clash at People First Stadium. While th...

item.title: Tanzania Shows Economic Resilience and Attracts Investors Despite Political Unrest
item.description: Even after the 2025 General Election unrest, Tanzania showed strong economic resilience, proving to the world that it is still a reliable destination for investment and tourism. Despite some planned v...

item.title: Zanzibar safely removes dangerous infant formula from market
item.description: The Zanzibar Food and Drugs Agency (ZFDA) has destroyed 4.8 tonnes of contaminated S.M.A No. 1 infant formula after it was found to contain cereulide, a harmful toxin produced by Bacillus cereus bacte...

item.title: Stella reveals her full history with Walter to Vincent in 'The Alibi' Episode 34
item.description: In the latest episode of the hit show 'The Alibi', Stella finally opened up to Vincent about her complicated past with Walter. The episode delves into the key events that shaped their relationship and...

item.title: Solomon Linda’s grave recognised as National Heritage Site for cultural significance
item.description: Sixty-six years after his passing, Solomon Linda, the composer behind the iconic song Mbube, has been honoured once more, this time with his grave at Doornkop Cemetery in Soweto declared a National He...

item.title: Study finds arginine reduces amyloid buildup and inflammation in Alzheimer’s animal models
item.description: A new study from researchers at Kindai University suggests that arginine, a common and inexpensive amino acid, may help reduce key features of Alzheimer’s disease in animal models. Alzheimer’s, a lead...

item.title: Gamestop proposes $5.6 billion acquisition of eBay, driving stock surge
item.description: Shares of eBay experienced a significant increase following the announcement that the U.S.-based Gamestop retail chain had made a takeover bid valued at $5.6 billion. This proposed acquisition represe...

item.title: Tanzania Teams Ready to Compete in Uganda African Rally Championship
item.description: Tanzania is bringing strong hopes to the Pearl of Africa Uganda Rally, scheduled from Wednesday to Saturday in Mbarara. Two crews representing the ASAS team will be competing: Randeep Singh with navig...

item.title: Tanzania Prepares 17 Swimmers for Africa Aquatics Championships in Algeria
item.description: Tanzania iko tayari kuwakilisha nchi yake vizuri kwenye Africa Aquatics Swimming Championships ya mwaka 2026, itakayofanyika kuanzia Mei 5 hadi 10 mjini Oran, Algeria. National Sports Council imewasha...

item.title: UNFPA Supports Expansion of Midwifery Education and Workforce in Pakistan
item.description: On the occasion of International Day of the Midwife on May 5, the United Nations Population Fund (UNFPA) reiterated its commitment to strengthen midwifery education and workforce development in Pakist...

item.title: Drone attack targets Fujairah oil facilities amid UAE-Iran tensions
item.description: On Monday, the Fujairah Oil Industry Zone in the UAE was struck by a drone attack allegedly from Iran, leading to fires and moderate injuries to three Indian workers. The UAE military reported success...

item.title: NSW introduces laws to criminalise secret GPS tracking used in domestic violence
item.description: The New South Wales government is set to introduce legislation making it a criminal offence to secretly monitor a person using GPS or surveillance devices, particularly in cases linked to domestic vio...

item.title: Dodoma Launches Four-Year Plan to Strengthen Protection for Women and Children
item.description: Dodoma Region has officially introduced a four-year Strategic Plan (2026/27–2029/30) aimed at ending violence against women and children, responding to growing social challenges linked to the region’s...
